title: acaena viridior
intro: Acaena viridior, also known as the small-leaf caterpillar plant or woolly-head, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the rose family and is native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Acaena viridior include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and burns, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Acaena viridior can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create a topical cream or ointment.
